metadata.title: Language Experience Repair & Adaptation for Digital Products | Xpandia
metadata.description: Xpandia helps SaaS, AI, EdTech, Product, Marketing, CX, and Localization teams repair, improve, and adapt high-impact Spanish and English content, product copy, documentation, and customer-facing experiences.
metadata.ogTitle: Language Experience Repair & Adaptation for Digital Products | Xpandia
metadata.ogDescription: Xpandia helps SaaS, AI, EdTech, Product, Marketing, CX, and Localization teams repair, improve, and adapt high-impact Spanish and English content, product copy, documentation, and customer-facing experiences.

hero.subheadline: Xpandia helps teams repair, improve, and adapt high-impact Spanish and English content, product experiences, documentation, and campaigns so they feel clear, natural, trustworthy, and ready for real users.
hero.body: We combine senior Spanish and English language expertise, localization judgment, UX awareness, and cultural adaptation to help your content work across the moments that matter: product use, onboarding, support, learning, marketing, and conversion.

proofPoints.title: Product-ready Spanish
proofPoints.desc: UI copy, onboarding, forms, errors, flows, and user-facing product content.

proofPoints.title: Content that connects
proofPoints.desc: Websites, campaigns, help centers, learning materials, and customer communication.

proofPoints.title: Audience-aware adaptation
proofPoints.desc: LatAm, US Hispanic, Spain, neutral Spanish, or specific regional markets.

positioning.headline: Translation helps users read. Adaptation helps users trust, understand, and act.
positioning.body1: A Spanish or English version can be technically correct and still feel foreign, literal, inconsistent, or disconnected from the audience. That gap affects clarity, trust, UX, conversion, support, learning, and product adoption.
positioning.body2: Language Experience Repair & Adaptation closes that gap. We help teams improve Spanish and English experiences that respect the product context, the user journey, the audience, the market, and the business goal.
positioning.callout: Our infrastructure is built exclusively for high-impact surfaces, where expert human judgment, deep context, and cultural adaptation are business-critical.
positioning.supporting: Our infrastructure is built exclusively for high-impact surfaces, where expert human judgment, deep context, and cultural adaptation are business-critical.

whatWeAdapt.headline: High-impact Spanish and English content across the user journey.
whatWeAdapt.intro: We focus on the language touchpoints your audience reads, follows, clicks, learns from, or uses to make a decision.

cards.title: Product and UX copy
cards.desc: Buttons, menus, onboarding, tooltips, notifications, forms, error messages.

cards.title: Websites and landing pages
cards.desc: Homepages, product pages, service pages, pricing pages, FAQs, lead-generation.

cards.title: Support and CX content
cards.desc: Help center articles, support macros, chatbot scripts, escalation language.

cards.title: Marketing and campaigns
cards.desc: Email campaigns, ads, launch messaging, sales content, value propositions.

cards.title: Learning and EdTech content
cards.desc: Lessons, scripts, quizzes, instructions, assessments, educational explanations.

cards.title: Terminology and tone
cards.desc: Preferred terms, consistency rules, tone guidance, style fixes, regional recommendations.

whenToUse.headline: Use it when Spanish/English exists, but does not yet feel made for the user.
items: Your Spanish/English content sounds literal, generic, inconsistent, or disconnected from the audience.
Your product UI, help content, or marketing copy needs to feel natural and trustworthy.
Your English-first product, website, content, or campaign needs an expert Spanish version or vice versa.
Your Spanish/English experience needs regional adaptation for LatAm, US Hispanic, Spain, or a specific market.
Your content needs to support conversion, clarity, trust, learning, or user action.
Your audience understands the words, but the experience still does not feel built for them.

coreSolutions.headline: Repair, adapt, and prepare Spanish/English experiences for real users.

cards.name: Language Experience Repair
cards.bestFor: Teams with existing Spanish or English content that sounds translated, inconsistent, unclear, generic, too literal, or not ready for real users.
cards.outcome: Turn underperforming language into content that feels clear, natural, trustworthy, and aligned with your product, audience, and business goal.

methodology.headline: A practical path from <u>translated content</u> to <u>audience-ready product</u>.

steps.title: Understand the context
steps.body: We clarify your audience, Spanish variant, product surface, content type, business goal, tone, terminology, and publication needs.

steps.title: Repair or adapt the experience
steps.body: We improve the selected content with attention to meaning, clarity, tone, terminology, regional fit, cultural fit, UX context, and user action.

steps.title: Review in context
steps.body: Where access is available, we review Spanish/English inside the product, website, document, flow, or layout so the copy works in the real experience.

steps.title: Deliver usable Spanish assets
steps.body: You receive adapted, QA-ready, or publication-ready Spanish/English copy, along with terminology guidance, regional fit notes, cultural rationale, and implementation comments.

steps.title: Prepare for launch or QA
steps.body: We recommend the right next step: publish, QA, sign off, repair further, expand, test, or move into Language Assurance for readiness validation.

finalCta.headline: Build Spanish/English your audience can understand, trust, and act on.
finalCta.body: Book a diagnostic call to review your content, product surface, audience, Spanish variant, timeline, and business goal. We'll recommend the right path: repair, adaptation, QA, sign-off, or ongoing support.
